title: monticalia subarachnoidea var. pauciflora
intro: Monticalia subarachnoidea var. pauciflora, also known as the 'few-flowered Monticalia', is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its small white flowers and edible roots. The medicinal properties of Monticalia subarachnoidea var. pauciflora lie in its roots, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems. The plant's roots have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it beneficial for improving overall health and well-being, such as aiding in digestion, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Monticalia subarachnoidea var. pauciflora can be made by infusing the roots in hot water to create a tea or by drying and powdering them for later use as a supplement.
